summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire and soaring jams make this Winterland show a solid treat
blurb: The concert shines with a fiery Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition and an expansive Caution (Do Not Stop) → Mojo jam. Jerry’s tone is sublime on Fire while Bob delivers passionate vocals in the second set. The drum solo launches a memorable Not Fade Away jam, rounding out a strong performance.
review: On October 22, 1978 at Winterland Arena the Dead delivered a night that still feels fresh for listeners today. The first set opened with an exuberant Ollin Arageed* and rolled through classics like Deal, Friend Of The Devil, and Peggy‑O, building steady momentum. The second set erupted when Scarlet Begonias slipped seamlessly into Fire On The Mountain—captured in astonishing three‑dimensional clarity on the Kempa soundboard release—and then surged through a thunderous drum solo that birthed an extended Not Fade Away jam. A highlight was the sprawling Caution (Do Not Stop) → Mojo improvisation, which kept the energy incandescent well into the encore of Johnny B. Goode. Across all available tapes the band feels locked in: Jerry’s lead work is both scorching and melodic, Phil’s bass dances with a lively feel, and Bob’s vocals drive the second‑set jams forward.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, highlighting standout songs and strong performances; the remaining six are mixed but not negative. This consistent overall praise yields a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard with a three‑dimensional mix; crystal‑clear capture of Scarlet>Fire and the Caution→Mojo jam makes it the definitive listening source.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence dazzles with crystal‑clear three‑dimensional sound.
Caution (Do Not Stop) → Mojo jam fuels an incandescent second set.
Friend Of The Devil and I Need A Miracle stand out in a strong first set.
Dynamic drum solo propels the extended Not Fade Away jam.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered a sublime, perfect‑tone lead on Fire and abundant scorching licks throughout; a few listeners felt his Scarlet Begonias was less inspired.
band_performance.Phil: Provided lively, dance‑like bass lines that energized the jams, especially evident in the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Strong, passionate vocal delivery in the second set and driving guitar work that helped launch the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: His drum solo acted as a catalyst for the Not Fade Away jam, adding dynamic power despite occasional low‑end distortion in high‑gain moments.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey supplied a powerful rhythmic foundation; the dual‑drum interplay fueled the extended improvisations.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
